name: Annexon, Inc.
business: Annexon, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company advancing targeted immunotherapies for complement-mediated neuroinflammatory diseases. It has three product candidates: tanruprubart for Guillain-Barré Syndrome (GBS), vonaprument for geographic atrophy (GA), and ANX1502 for autoimmune conditions. None of its product candidates are approved for sale, and the company has not generated any revenue from product sales.

exposures.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company advancing the next generation platform of targeted immunotherapies aimed at complement-mediated neuroinflammatory diseases that impact nearly 10 million people worldwide.

catalysts.event: Filed MAA with EMA for tanruprubart in GBS
catalysts.sentence: We filed the Marketing Authorization Application, or MAA, with the European Medicines Agency, or EMA, for tanruprubart for the treatment of GBS in January 2026.

catalysts.event: Plan to submit BLA to FDA for tanruprubart in GBS
catalysts.sentence: We plan to submit a biologics license application, or BLA, to the FDA for GBS in 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline data from ARCHER II Phase 3 trial for vonaprument
catalysts.sentence: We plan to report topline data in the fourth quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Update on ANX1502 POC study completion
catalysts.sentence: we plan to provide an update upon study completion in 2026.

tamClaims.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company advancing the next generation platform of targeted immunotherapies aimed at complement-mediated neuroinflammatory diseases that impact nearly 10 million people worldwide.

Tanruprubart is an investigational targeted immunotherapy delivered in a single infusion to rapidly halt aggressive neuroinflammation and damage in GBS, an acute, rare, neuromuscular emergency that annually affects ~150,000 people worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: more than eight million people worldwide for GA
tamClaims.sentence: Vonaprument is an investigational neuroprotective inhibitor of C1q and the classical complement cascade delivered intravitreally for GA, a leading cause of blindness affecting more than eight million people worldwide.
